recalibration: Quarterly: Full recalibration using prior quarter’s operational data (exceptions, near-misses, false positives, override patterns). Monthly: Threshold sensitivity check — are escalation volumes appropriate? (Target: 5-15% of actions generate exceptions.) Real-time: If false positive rate exceeds 25% in any dimension for 7 consecutive days, trigger automatic threshold review. Annual: Board-level review of BOSS model integrity with external validation.

exception_economy.packet_structure: Every exception packet contains: 1) The original Intent Object (full schema). 2) The proposed action and execution plan. 3) Per-dimension BOSS scores with policy provenance. 4) The specific dimension(s) that triggered escalation. 5) Predicted impact analysis (financial, regulatory, reputational). 6) Alternative approaches with their BOSS scores. 7) Recommended decision with confidence level. 8) Time sensitivity (how long can this wait?). 9) Link to related historical exceptions for pattern context. 10) One-click approve/reject/modify interface.
exception_economy.target_volume: Target: 5-15 exceptions per director per day across all domains. Warning threshold: >20 exceptions/day for any single director suggests thresholds are too conservative or the domain needs additional delegation capacity. Critical: >30/day triggers automatic threshold relaxation for low-risk exception categories + alert to CEO. ADAM tracks escalation frequency, resolution time, and override patterns to continuously optimize this balance.
exception_economy.feedback_loop: Every resolved exception is stored in the Flight Recorder with: the original exception, the human decision, the reasoning (if provided), and the outcome. Monthly analysis: Exception patterns are clustered by type. If a class of exceptions is approved >90% of the time without modification, the system proposes a threshold adjustment to auto-approve similar future cases. If a class is rejected >50%, the system proposes a tighter constraint. Proposed doctrine changes are presented to the relevant director as a structured proposal — ADAM never self-amends doctrine.
